channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Cursor’s New Tool Lets Users Delegate to a Team of Coding Agents
item.description: That's one way to move into a management role.

item.title: Nvidia Needs to Remind Itself What PC Gamers Actually Want
item.description: Even more frame generation, aka 'fake frames,' will be less impactful than a patch to boost load times.

item.title: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers says its customer support system was hacked
item.description: The U.S. telehealth giant says hackers stole customer support ticket data over the course of several days in February.

item.title: The best AirPods deals you can get right now
item.description: If you know where to look, you can often score deals on Apple’s ever-expanding AirPods lineup. Both the AirPods Pro 3 and the AirPods 4 (with and without ANC) now consistently receive discounts, as do the AirPods Pro 3. And while major shopping events like Black Friday and Amazon Prime Day have delivered some of the […]

item.title: Iran Says It Hit Oracle Facilities in UAE
item.description: After Amazon, Oracle is now in the bulls-eye.

item.title: VR game Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Empire City launches on April 30
item.description: Everyone's four favorite anthropomorphic turtles are returning to the world of video games. Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Empire City will be released on April 30 for the Meta Quest, Steam VR and Pico. It is made by VR game company Cortopia Studios and will retail for $25. Empire City is a first-person action game that you’ll be able to play solo or co-op with up to four people. And yes, that means all four of the turtles are playable. We've seen a lot of the quartet flexing their fighting form in games over the years, but this is their first time appearing in a standalone VR title. In additio...

item.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran’s Threats on US Tech, Trump’s Plans for Midterms, and Polymarket’s Pop-up Flop
item.description: In this episode, we discuss Iran’s threats to target US tech firms, gear up for the midterm elections, and get a scene report from the Polymarket pop-up bar in DC.

item.title: ‘Invincible’ Season 4 Is Doing Right By Debbie
item.description: The Prime Video superhero animated series' heated reunion between J.K. Simmons' Omni-Man and Sandra Oh's Debbie Grayson ate and left no crumbs.

item.title: The Google Pixel 10 Is $150 Off
item.description: Amazon marks Google's flagship down to just $650 for 128 GB or $750 for 256 GB.

item.title: Tech Companies Are Trying to Neuter Colorado’s Landmark Right-to-Repair Law
item.description: A bill in Colorado is a glimpse into the future of how corporations are working to limit the freedom people have to make their own fixes and upgrades.

item.title: 'Unlimited 5G data': HP's latest AI PC ultraslim laptop promises superfast WiFi-killer broadband and built-in antivirus — just make sure you read the fine print
item.description: HP Go 5G provides “unlimited” data under restrictive terms, with prepaid plans, throttling, and limited international access.

item.title: New Theory Says We’ve Been Overcomplicating the Big Bang
item.description: The lens of quantum physics offers a much simpler view of the Big Bang, research suggests.

item.title: Artemis II is NASA’s last moon mission without Silicon Valley
item.description: Next time around, the pressure will be on SpaceX and Blue Origin.

item.title: This bike rack pioneer is selling Bluetooth suction cups to stick bikes to your car
item.description: Richard Allen didn't invent the automobile bike rack - his 1967 patent application makes it clear that others came before. But after nearly sixty years selling popular and simple mechanical bike carriers, his company Allen Bikes now offers a line of - yes - Bluetooth-monitored suction cups to stick bikes to your car. If you […]

item.title: Marvel Might Have Finally Dealt With One of the Most Hated ‘Spider-Man’ Characters Around
item.description: After a few weeks of suffering for Spidey fans, the lord publisher giveth, and the lord publisher taketh away.

item.title: China Is Trying to Grow New Forests in This Notorious Mega-Desert. It’s Working
item.description: Independent researchers in the U.S. have confirmed that China has managed to create a new carbon sink out of portions of Earth’s second-largest ‘mobile desert.’

item.title: You can track Artemis II in real time as Orion flies to the moon
item.description: NASA has released a tracker website and app for people to follow the Artemis II mission, a 10-day test flight around the moon and back.

item.title: The ABS Challenge System is exposing the worst umpire in baseball
item.description: During Wednesday's game between the Tampa Bay Rays and the Milwaukee Brewers, umpire CB Bucknor took a foul ball to the mask and had to be helped off the field. It was the cap to what has been a particularly bad week for one of the most controversial umpires in baseball. It started with perhaps […]

item.title: Gateway Capital announces first close of $25M Fund II
item.description: Gateway Capital, the Milwaukee-based venture firm founded by Dana Guthrie, can now begin investment operations for its $25M Fund II.

item.title: RAM Prices Are Threatening the Viability of the Raspberry Pi and Single-Board Computing
item.description: The random access meltdown rumbles on.
